texto: I had just left 'Saturday Night Live' when I came to 'The Daily Show,' and it just felt like Jon was on my side. I'll always be grateful to him for that. I just got the impression he wanted me to succeed, and then I wanted to succeed for him. I think that's good leadership.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Rob Riggle: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “I had just left 'Saturday Night Live' when I came to 'The Daily Show,' and it just felt like Jon …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que lideranca, daily, good pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

texto: I think audiences can relate to the guys I'm playing, those large-and-in-charge idiots. Or maybe I just make them feel better about who they are, or who they're dating.

texto: Just take yourself back to September 2001. As that month went on, and as that autumn unrolled, everybody wanted to do something. How can I help? Do you want my blood? Do you want money for the victims? I was a captain in the Marine Corps. I knew what I could do. I was right there. I was ready. I saw it. I moved the rubble with my own hands.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Rob Riggle que provoca temas ligados a negocios. Ao questionar “Just take yourself back to September 2001. As that month went on, and as that autumn unrolled, ev…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre negocios, autumn, yourself, com foco em yourself e help.

texto: I wasn't the sharpest tool in the shed, and I wasn't the biggest, so I got real good at running my mouth and making people laugh and using humor as a way to not get into fights.
